notes: Japanese grammar contains the full grammar-library index captured from Bunpro's public grammar list metadata; it does not copy premium example sentences or full lesson prose.
Cantonese grammar is a structured teaching index synthesized from Jason Tang's Guide to Cantonese Grammar and CUHK's Cantonese Grammar multimedia chapter structure, with concise original summaries.
Vocabulary files are normalized from the two CSV files supplied by the user.
Abstract chapter-label grammar entries with no Japanese or Cantonese surface form were removed from the learner-facing dataset.
Sentence and passage comprehension items are original AIDA learning content added for target-aware comprehension practice.
Passage comprehension now uses typed-response assessment with reference-answer comparison and learner-confirmed grading; the local match score is advisory only.
An additional original reading passage bank is stored in data/reading_passages.js.
Context coverage is exhaustive at runtime: every vocabulary and grammar item can generate sentence variations and a mini-passage; imported corpus examples are preferred when available.
Selected Japanese context examples were imported from Tatoeba/Tanaka-WWWJDIC data; selected Cantonese conversational examples come from HKCanCor via PyCantonese.
V7 study sessions stratify across all allowed levels and order selected material from easier to harder.
V7 Usage Lab converts common Japanese romaji to kana and uses global dynamic-programming segmentation with contextual candidate ranking instead of greedy local matching.
V7 Cantonese audio recognizes yue-CN, yue-HK, other yue locales, and zh-HK, with manual browser voice selection.

V7 learner-facing Cantonese vocabulary excludes source rows with no definition, explicit deletion/editing markers, or no usable pronunciation; excluded rows are preserved in exports/cantonese_vocabulary_excluded.csv.
Every learner-facing Cantonese vocabulary item now has both a non-empty meaning and a pronunciation/Jyutping field.
All 963 Japanese grammar items now have at least three non-meta context examples: corpus examples where available and original AIDA grammar examples for the 221 items with no imported corpus match.
Imported Japanese grammar items with only one or two corpus examples are padded to three by contextual variations of the corpus sentence rather than meta-linguistic placeholder cards.

V7 provides three progressive sentence contexts and three progressive mini-passage variations for every active vocabulary and grammar source item; repeated encounters advance through easier, build, and harder variants.

Cantonese pronunciation data is normalized for learner display; all active vocabulary items and all displayed bundled Cantonese examples have usable pronunciation/Jyutping, while unrecoverable source rows remain in the excluded audit CSV.
V7 includes an optional same-origin Vercel serverless Cantonese TTS endpoint backed by Azure Speech; browser-native yue-CN/yue-HK/zh-HK remains the no-key fallback.
V9 replaces concatenated mini-passages with a coherent scenario engine that embeds the target context in a semantic-domain setup, development, and consequence.
V9 tracks recognition, production, listening, reading, and grammar as independent memory skills.
V9 vendors ts-fsrs and schedules each practiced item-skill memory independently with configurable desired retention.
V9 adds transcript-hidden sentence and passage listening practice with synchronized reveal highlighting and Cantonese ruby Jyutping.

sources.cantonese_vocabulary_source: user-supplied Most Common Cantonese Words (Frequency List) - 40kExport.csv.csv
sources.japanese_context_corpus: https://tatoeba.org/en/downloads (WWWJDIC Japanese-English export subset used in build)
